title: Confirm exact Clear Filters confirmation copy

decision.question: Should the Clear Filters confirmation copy explicitly say that active Jobs results will broaden after confirmation?
decision.whyItMatters: The action changes active browse constraints, so vague confirmation copy could make users think only modal-local edits are being cleared.
decision.decisionNeededBy: Before marking the desired Clear Filters behavior as confirmed governing.
blockedBehavior: Exact confirmation copy cannot be marked governing.
Implementation may proceed only with non-governing placeholder copy if behavior is otherwise confirmed.

options.summary: Use copy that explicitly says active filters will be cleared and results will broaden.
options.tradeoffsMd: Best for clarity, but slightly longer confirmation text.

options.summary: Use short copy that only asks whether the user wants to clear filters.
options.tradeoffsMd: More concise, but easier to confuse with modal-local cleanup.

consequencesIfUnresolved: Generated review docs should show the copy decision as pending.
Tests should avoid asserting exact confirmation text until confirmed.

recommendation.rationaleMd: The copy should resolve the main ambiguity between modal-local cleanup and active browse reset.

alternatives.tradeoffsMd: Acceptable only if the surrounding UI already makes the reset scope obvious.

recommendation.consequencesMd: If accepted, mark exact confirmation copy as governing for the Clear Filters requirement.

confirmation.notesMd: This pending decision governs exact copy only, not the broader Clear Filters behavior.

confirmationBaseline.notesMd: Backfilled as the audit baseline for the one-pass UX spec creation/update. This records provenance only; it does not replace UX authority, product confirmation, behavior mappings, or runtime drift ledgers.
